mission.title: Install and Configure Inclavare Containers on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inclavare Containers is a novel container runtime designed for confidential computing, providing hardware-enforced isolation for sensitive workloads. Installing Inclavare Containers allows you to run protected containers in a Trusted Execution Environment, ensuring your data remains secure from untrusted entities.

steps.title: Add the Inclavare Containers Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Inclavare Containers Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration to access the installation charts.
```bash
helm repo add inclavare-containers https://charts.inclavare-containers.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Inclavare Containers using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy Inclavare Containers to your Kubernetes cluster using the Helm chart, specifying the namespace for installation.
```bash
helm install inclavare-containers inclavare-containers/inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare --create-namespace --version v0.6.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Inclavare Containers pods are running successfully in the specified namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace inclavare
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Inclavare Containers
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Inclavare Containers deployment to ensure optimal performance and resource allocation.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"inclavare-containers","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}'
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Inclavare Containers
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow the necessary permissions for Inclavare Containers to function correctly.
```bash
kubectl create role inclavare-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace inclavare
kubectl create rolebinding inclavare-rolebinding --role=inclavare-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace inclavare
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Inclavare Containers will show the pods running in the 'inclavare' namespace. You should also see the resource limits applied and the RBAC permissions set correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add inclavare-containers https://charts.inclavare-containers.io
helm repo update
helm install inclavare-containers inclavare-containers/inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare --create-namespace --version v0.6.2

kubectl create role inclavare-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace inclavare
kubectl create rolebinding inclavare-rolebinding --role=inclavare-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace inclavare

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
